auth_notes: Authentication is handled by passing an 'access_token' and 'instance_id' as query parameters or within the JSON body of the HTTP request.
rate_limits: Rate limits are not explicitly documented but are constrained by the WhatsApp instance's capacity and anti-spam measures to prevent account banning.
gotchas: WhatsApp account disconnection requires manual QR code re-scanning, which can break automated workflows.
High frequency of messages can lead to WhatsApp account bans as this is a third-party API wrapper.
API responses may occasionally be in Portuguese or poorly translated English.
client_must_provide: Active WhatsApp account linked via QR code
research_notes: Integration requires the n8n HTTP Request node. The developer must construct the URL using the format 'https://api.5-instances.com/api/v1/sendText?token=YOUR_TOKEN&instance_id=YOUR_ID'. Webhooks must be configured in the 5-instances dashboard to point to an n8n Webhook node for receiving incoming messages.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Integration requires the n8n HTTP Request node. The developer must construct the URL using the format 'https://api.5-instances.com/api/v1/sendText?token=YOUR_TOKEN&instance_id=YOUR_ID'.
